title: Agrochemical composition with improved drift and spreading properties
abstract: The present invention relates to agrochemical compositions: their use for foliar application; their use at low spray volumes; their use by unmanned aerial systems (UAS), unmanned guided vehicles (UGV), and tractor mounted boom sprayers fitted with conventional nozzles but also pulse width modulation spray nozzles or rotating disc droplet applicators; and their application for controlling agricultural pests, weeds or diseases, in particular on waxy leaves, and in particular the present invention relates to agrochemical compositions with a reduced drift, in particular in spray applications.
claims:
Claims -86-Claims 1. Agrochemical formulation comprising a) One or more active ingredients, b) One or more drift reducing ingredients c) One or more spreading agents, f) Other formulants, g) one or more carriers to volume, comprising the components a) to c) in the following amounts a) from 5 to 500 g/1, , b) from 0.01 to 50 g/1, and in case of b) being an vegetable oil or ester from in 1 to 50 g/1, in case of b) being a drift reducing polymer in 0.05 to 3 g/1, , c) from 5 to 150 g/1, g) carrier to volume . 2. Agrochemical formulation according to claim 1, wherein component f) is mandatory and present in fl) from 8 to 120 g/1, f2) from 1 to 20 g/1, f3) from 0.5 to 20 g/1, f4) from 5 to 150 g/1, , f5) from 0.1 to 120 g/1, 3. Agrochemical formulation according to claim 1 or 2, wherein b) is selected from the group comprising poly(ethylene) oxides with an average molecular weight preferably from 0.5 to 12 million g/mol, more preferred from 0.75 to 10 million g/mol, and most preferred from 1 to 8 million g/mol, and vegetable oils and vegetable oil esters and diester including esters with glycerine and propylene glycol. 4. Agrochemical formulation according to claim 1 or 2, wherein b) is selected from the group comprising a polymer selected from the group consisting of hydroxypropyl guar and poly(ethylene) oxides with an average molecular weight preferably from 0.5 to 12 million g/mol, more preferred from 0.75 to 10 million g/mol, and most preferred from 1 to 8 million g/mol, and is present in 0.05 to 10 g/1, preferably from 0.1 to 8 g/1, and most preferred from 0.2 to 6 g/1., more preferred a poly (ethylene oxide). 

 -87- 5. Agrochemical formulation according to claim 1 or 2, wherein b) is selected from the group comprising vegetable oils and vegetable oil esters and diester including esters with glycerine and propylene glycol and is present 1 to 50 g/1, preferably from 5 to 30 g/1, and most preferred from 6 to 30 g/1- 6. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 5, wherein c) is selected from the group comprising polyalkyleneoxide modified heptamethyltrisiloxanes, dioctylsulfosuccinate, alcohol ethoxylates and ethoxylated diacetylene-diols with 1 to 6 EO, preferably from the group comprising polyalkyleneoxide modified heptamethyltrisiloxanes, dioctylsulfosuccinate and ethoxylated diacetylene-diols with 1 to 6 EO. 7. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 6, wherein a) is present in an amount from 10 to 320 g/1, and most preferred from 20 to 230 g/1. 8. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 7, wherein c) is present in from 10 to 120 g/1, and most preferred from 20 to 80 g/1. 9. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 8, wherein b) present according to claim 5 is applied from 0. 1 g/ha to 50 g/ha, more preferably from 1 g/ha to 40 g/ha, and most preferred from 5 g/ha to 30 g/ha. 10. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 8, wherein b) present according to claim 4 is applied from 0.01 g/ha to 25 g/ha, more preferably from 0.05 g/ha to 10 g/ha, and most preferred from 0.1 g/ha to 6 g/ha. 11. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 10, wherein the active ingredient is selected from the group consisting of trifloxystrobin, bixafen, penflufen, tebuconazole, prothioconazole, inpyrfluxam, isoflucypram, fluopicolide, fluopyram, fluoxapiprolin, spirotetramat, tetraniliprole, ethiprole, imidacloprid, deltamethrin, flupyradifuron, spidoxamat, triafamone, tembotrione, thiencarbazone-methyl, isoxadifen-ethyl and cyprosulfamat. 12. Agrochemical formulation according to one or more of claims 1 to 11, wherein component f) comprises at least one non-ionic surfactant and / or ionic surfactant (fl), one rheological modifier (f2) , and one antifoam substance (f3) and at least one antifreeze agent (f4). 13. Agrochemical formulation according to claim any one of claims 1 to 12, comprising the components a) to g) in the following amounts a) from 5 to 500 g/1, preferably from 10 to 320 g/1, and most preferred from 20 to 230 g/1, b) from 0.01 to 50 g/1, preferably from 0.1 to 30 g/1, and most preferred from 1 to 20 g/1, and in case of b) being an vegetable oil or ester from in 1 to 50 g/1, preferably from 5 to 30 g/1, and most preferred from 6 to 30 g/1, in case of b) being a drift reducing polymer in 0.05 to 3 g/1, preferably from 0. 1 to 2 g/1, and most preferred from 0.2 to 1.5 g/1 c) from 5 to 150 g/1, preferably from 10 to 120 g/1, and most preferred from 20 to 80 g/1, fl) from 4 to 250 g/1, preferably from 8 to 120 g/1, and most preferred from 10 to 80 g/1, f2) from 0 to 60 g/1, preferably from 1 to 20 g/1, and most preferred from 2 to 10 g/1, 

 -88- f3) from 0 to 30 g/1, preferably from 0.5 to 20 g/1, and most preferred from 1 to 12 g/1, f4) from 0 to 200 g/1, preferably from 5 to 150 g/1, and most preferred from 10 to 120 g/1, f5) from 0 to 200 g/1, preferably from 0.1 to 120 g/1, and most preferred from 0.5 to 80 g/1, g) carrier to volume . 14. Agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13, wherein the formulation is an in-can formulation 15. Agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13, wherein the concentration of the additives b) to c) in the spray liquid is from Additive b) from 0.005 to 1 g/1, and most preferred from 0.04 to 0.6 g/1 where b) is a polymer Additive b) from 0.01 to 5 g/1, and most preferred from 0.02 to 2.5 g/1 where b) is an oil Additive c) from 0.25 to 5 g/1, and most preferred from 1 to 3 g/1. 16. Agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13, wherein the dose of the additives b) to c) per ha is from Additive b) from 0.05 to 10 g/ha, and most preferred from 0.4 to 6 g/ha where b) is a polymer Additive b) from 0.1 to 50 g/ha, and most preferred from 0.2 to 30 g/ha where b) is an oil Additive c) from 1.25 to 50 g/ha, and most preferred from 10 to 30 g/ha. 17. Agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13, wherein the concentration in the formulation, the concentration in the spray liquid and the dose of the additives b) to c) per ha is combined in the following way Additive b) from 0.4 to 6 g/1 in the formulation, from 0.02 to 0.6 g/1 in the spray liquid and from 0.2 to 6 g/ha where b) is a polymer Additive b) from 0. 1 to 50 g/1 in the formulation, from 0.01 to 5 g/1 in the spray liquid and from 0.2 to 30 g/ha where b) is an oil Additive c) from 10 to 40 g/1 in the formulation, from 0.5 to 4 g/1 in the spray liquid and from 8 to 30 g/ha 

 -89- 18. Agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13, wherein the formulation is applied at a spray volume of between land 30 1/ha, preferably 1 and 20 1/ha, more preferably 2 and 15 1/ha, most preferably 5 and 15 1/ha. 19. Method of applying an agrochemical composition according to one or more claims 1 to 13 onto crops, wherein the formulation is applied at a spray volume of between land 30 1/ha, preferably 1 and 20 1/ha, more preferably 2 and 15 1/ha, most preferably 5 and 15 1/ha.. 20. Use of an agrochemical composition according to one or more of the claims 1 to 13 in application of the agrochemical compounds for controlling harmful organisms, wherein the composition is applied by a UAV, UGV, PWM.
